vdb_search_docs.name: vdb_search_docs
vdb_search_docs.description: Document-level semantic retrieval from the vector store. Use for broad topical recall when you need a few candidate passages or documents related to the question. Good for coarse exploration, but usually weaker than `vdb_search_hierdocs` or `vdb_search_sentences` for precise QA evidence.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Semantic search query describing the information need.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of document-level hits to return. Default 5.

vdb_get_docs_by_document_ids.name: vdb_get_docs_by_document_ids
vdb_get_docs_by_document_ids.description: Fetch raw document chunks by document_id from the vector store. Use it after another tool has already produced source document_ids and you need the underlying text for grounding or follow-up reading.

parameters.name: document_ids
parameters.description: List of document_ids whose chunks should be returned.

vdb_search_hierdocs.name: vdb_search_hierdocs
vdb_search_hierdocs.description: Parent-child semantic retrieval: recall relevant sentences, aggregate them to parent passages/documents, then rerank. Use it when wording may be paraphrased, when several answer options are semantically close, or when you need broader context around matched evidence. It is usually better for broad semantic grounding than for resolving whose opinion or relationship is being discussed.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Semantic search query describing the evidence you need.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of final parent-level hits to return. Default 20.

vdb_search_sentences.name: vdb_search_sentences
vdb_search_sentences.description: Sentence-level semantic retrieval for fine-grained evidence. Strong when the answer depends on a specific sentence, paraphrased statement, short fact, reason, attitude, implication, or other local detail and the exact wording is unknown. Do not rely on it alone when the question hinges on whose opinion, relationship, or claim is meant; pair it with lexical or section-grounded retrieval in those cases.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Semantic sentence-search query. Phrase it as the fact or evidence you want to find.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of sentence hits to return. Default 5.
